IPsec ESP Inbound (gxcr/tokens/esp_ip4v_tunnel_ibound.h)
[IPsec API (gxcr/ipsec.h)]

Detailed Description

The ESP IPv4 Tunneling Inbound token decapsulates an IPv4 packet.

It strips off the outer IP header, validates the ESP header's SPI and sequence number against a specified SA, decrypts the inner IPv4 packet, removes and validates any padding, and compares an ICV calculated on the packet against the ICV at the end of the packet.

Before packets are processed with this interface, basic data structures need to be initialized at the gxcr_ipsec level. See ipsec.h. This is the level at which IPsec parameters such as the encryption and authentication algorithms and keys are specified.

Certain parameters are determined at Security Association setup time, others are not known until a particular packet is being processed. The function ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_setup() sets up the SA-specific data. This function must be called once, at initialization time, for each Security Association.

The setup function leaves as little work as possible for the runtime function, ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet() or ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet_start(), to perform for each packet. The runtime function expects a packet in the same form as that output by the ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound token generates. It validates the SPI and sequence number against the SA, updates the replay window in the SA, and compares the hash performed on the packet against the trailing ICV. The result packet is an unencrypted IPv4 packet with an updated header.

See this example program for an illustration of how to use this API (along with its outbound conterpart): mica/ipsec/appc : IPsec ESP/IPv4/Tunneling Outbound and Inbound packet processing.

Data Structures

struct  ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t
 Data structure that encapsulates general IPsec SA and operation-specific parameters. More...

Functions

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_setup (ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *op_sa, int outer_ip_hdr_len, int esp_hdr_len)
 Function to set up for inbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing.
int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet_start (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *op_sa, void *packet, int packet_len, void *dst, int dst_len)
 Function to perform inbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.
int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *op_sa, void *packet, int packet_len, void *dst, int dst_len)
 Function to perform inbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.
int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_minimum_dst_len (ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *op_sa, int src_len)
 Function to calculate the minimum length of the output (destination) buffer for functions: ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet_start or ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet.

Variables

gxcr_token_info_t esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound
 Token for ESP/IPv4/Tunneling Inbound packet processing.

Function Documentation

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_setup ( ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *  op_sa,
int  outer_ip_hdr_len,
int  esp_hdr_len 
)

Function to set up for inbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing.

Parameters:
op_sa - An IPsec SA initialized via gxcr_ipsec_init_sa().
outer_ip_hdr_len - Length, in bytes, of the IP tunneling header expected on inbound packet.
esp_hdr_len - Length, in bytes, of the ESP header expected in inbound packets.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet_start ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *  op_sa,
void *  packet,
int  packet_len,
void *  dst,
int  dst_len 
)

Function to perform inbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.

This function returns immediately.

Parameters:
mica_context - An initialized MiCA context.
op_sa - An initialized operation-specific SA.
packet - The packet to be processed.
packet_len - Length, in bytes, of the packet to be processed.
dst - Destination memory for the result packet. If dst is equal to packet, the memory pointed to by packet is overwritten. The memory pointed to by packet and by dst must not overlap otherwise.
dst_len - Length, in bytes, of destination memory for the result packet.
Returns:
0 on success, error code on failure.

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *  op_sa,
void *  packet,
int  packet_len,
void *  dst,
int  dst_len 
)

Function to perform inbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.

This function blocks until completion.

Parameters:
mica_context - An initialized MiCA context.
op_sa - An initialized operation-specific SA.
packet - The packet to be processed. If dst is equal to packet, the memory pointed to by packet is overwritten. The memory pointed to by packet and by dst must not overlap otherwise.
packet_len - Length, in bytes, of the packet to be processed.
dst - Destination memory for the result packet.
dst_len - Length, in bytes, of destination memory for the result packet.
Returns:
0 on success, error code on failure.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_minimum_dst_len ( ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_t *  op_sa,
int  src_len 
)

Function to calculate the minimum length of the output (destination) buffer for functions: ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet_start or ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_process_packet.

Calls to those functions supplying an output buffer whose size, as given by the dst_len parameter, is smaller than the value returned by this function will fail.

Parameters:
op_sa An initialized operation-specific SA.
src_len Length, in bytes, of the packet to be processed.
Returns:
The minimum output data length in bytes.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.

Variable Documentation

gxcr_token_info_t esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound

Token for ESP/IPv4/Tunneling Inbound packet processing.

Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.
